turns.content: What brings you in today?

Hi, thanks for seeing me. So, I woke up yesterday morning with this really itchy rash on both of my arms. It came out of nowhere, and it's been keeping me up at night because it just won't stop itching.
When did this start?

It started yesterday morning, right when I woke up. I didn't have it the night before when I went to bed, but it was there as soon as I looked at my arms. It's been getting itchier throughout the day, and last night was the worst.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

It's mostly just really intense itching, not really a sharp pain—more like a burning, irritating sensation. The itching is worst on my forearms, but I can feel it up to my upper arms too. Sometimes when I scratch, it stings a bit.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, I haven't had any fever or chills. I don't feel sick otherwise, just really annoyed by the itching. The rash itself feels a bit warm to the touch, though, especially where I've been scratching.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not on any prescription medications right now. I do take an over-the-counter antihistamine sometimes, but I haven't taken any in the past few days. Oh, and I occasionally use ibuprofen for headaches, but not recently.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, I don't have any chronic conditions. I'm generally pretty healthy, aside from the occasional cold or headache. This rash is really unusual for me.
Are you allergic to any medications?
I'm not aware of any allergies to medications. I've never had a bad reaction to anything I've taken, so I don't think so. But I've also never taken penicillin, so I just don't know for sure.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Yeah, I've tried a few things. I put some over-the-counter anti-itch cream on it last night, but it didn't really help that much. I also tried a cold compress, which felt good for a few minutes, but the itching came back as soon as I took it off. I've been trying not to scratch, but honestly, it's really hard.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Warmth definitely makes it worse—like when I took a shower yesterday, the hot water just made it flare up even more. I also noticed that being outside in the sun yesterday seemed to irritate it. The only thing that gives me a little relief is keeping my arms cool, like with the cold compress or just rolling up my sleeves in an air-conditioned room.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been pretty hard to focus on my schoolwork because I'm constantly distracted by the itching. I had a study session yesterday and I just couldn't sit still. Plus, not sleeping well last night made me really tired and irritable today. It's honestly just taken over my whole day.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No, I don't smoke at all. I do drink alcohol occasionally—maybe a drink or two at a party or with friends—but I haven't had anything in the past week.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
Not that I know of. My parents and siblings don't have any skin conditions or allergies that I'm aware of. No one in my family has had a rash like this before.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, never. I've had a few small rashes before from bug bites or something, but nothing like this. This one covers both arms and is so much itchier than anything I've had before. It's really weird for me.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say the itching is about a 6 out of 10—it's really distracting and uncomfortable, but not completely unbearable. Emotionally, it's probably around a 7, because I'm just so frustrated and anxious about not sleeping and not knowing what's causing it. It's really getting to me.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

I don't think so—you've asked about everything I can think of. Oh, actually, now that I'm thinking about it, I did start using a new laundry detergent last week, and I also brushed against some plants in my friend's garden a few days ago. I don't know if either of those could be related, but it's all I can think of.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Honestly, I'm just trying to push through it. I've been using the cold compresses and trying really hard not to scratch, but it's exhausting. I've also been wearing long sleeves to cover it up so I'm not tempted to look at it and keep scratching. I guess I'm just hoping you can tell me what it is so I can actually get some relief and focus on my classes again.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I already mentioned it, but really I'm just using cold compresses and trying not to scratch. It's been hard, especially at night, but I'm managing. I guess I'm just hoping you can help me figure this out so I can get some sleep and stop being so irritable.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

Sorry, I know I just said this, but honestly, it's the same. Cold compresses and willpower, basically. I've also been trying to keep myself busy so I don't think about the itch, but it's really hard to ignore.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I know I keep saying the same thing, but it's just been the cold compresses and trying not to scratch. Honestly, it's wearing me down because I'm not sleeping and it's hard to concentrate. Sorry if I'm repeating myself, I'm just really tired and frustrated at this point.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I'm sorry, I think I've already answered that a few times now—it's just the cold compresses and trying not to scratch. Is there something else you're looking for? I'm getting a bit confused, sorry. I just really want to know what's going on with my skin.
